title: pygeum coccineum
intro: Pygeum coccineum, also known as the red stinkwood or African plum, is a plant species that belongs to the Proteaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains a compound called lupeol that has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of pygeum coccineum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH), improve urinary flow, and reduce the risk of prostate cancer. Pygeum coccineum is often used in herbal preparations such as capsules, tablets, and tinctures, as well as in traditional medicine in Africa.
